steps.title: Identify which traits distinguish species with broken-wing display
steps.content: Species performing the display (warbler and dove) have shorter incubation periods (13–15 days) and greater breeding capacity (3–7 broods per year), while species without the display (teal and oystercatcher) have longer incubation (25–28 days) and fewer broods (1 each). The number of parent incubators does not follow this pattern.

steps.title: Match the answer to the observed associations
steps.content: Choice A directly identifies incubation duration and brood capacity as more strongly associated with the behavior than parental numbers—the exact pattern in the data. The other choices either reverse the relationship, deny the pattern's differential strength, or misread which species have greater reproductive opportunity. That matches choice A — A is correct.
